lx24.title: id prefix doesn't include complete items
lx24.body:
## Log
- 2026-02-19: Fixed ID prefix highlighting to include completed items

## Implementation
- Modified `calculate_unique_prefixes` in `src/commands/list.rs` to use all changes from the database when computing unique prefixes
- This ensures the list view shows the correct minimum unique prefix even when completed items are hidden
- Fixed pre-existing test compilation issues (using `ID` instead of `Id`)
- Fixed pre-existing clippy warnings in other files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] ID prefix calculation in list view includes completed items
- [x] The unique prefix shown in the UI correctly reflects the minimum needed to disambiguate from all changes
- [x] All tests pass
- [x] Code compiles without warnings

2p4t.title: abc
2p4t.body:
## Implementation Notes

Implemented unique prefix highlighting for change IDs in the 'pebbles show' command.

### Changes Made:
1. Added 'unique_prefix_len()' function in src/commands/mod.rs:74 to calculate the minimum prefix length needed to uniquely identify a change ID among all others
2. Added 'format_id_with_unique_prefix()' function in src/commands/mod.rs:96 to format the ID with the unique prefix in bold cyan and the rest dimmed
3. Updated 'show' command in src/commands/show.rs:20-26 to use the new formatting

### Technical Details:
- The unique prefix is calculated by comparing the target ID against all other change IDs
- The shortest prefix that makes the ID unique is highlighted
- Format: unique prefix in bold cyan, remaining characters dimmed
- Example: If change '2p4t' is the only one starting with '2', it will display as **2**p4t (with '2' bold and 'p4t' dimmed)

## Log
- 2026-02-18: Implemented unique prefix highlighting for change IDs

1dut.title: Provide Answers Workflow
1dut.body:
## Goal

If a plan has a list of questions, then this is a way for the human to provide answers to the LLM.

```sh
pebbles answer <IDish>
```

the idea is that we read the questions out to the user, and let them provide answers.

Starting with just one pass at a time. Present X questions, the user responds, we save that, update the plan.
generate new questions if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Reads the question section, and present it to the user
- [ ] Reads in user answers, updates the plan, and then writes any new questions
- [ ] saves and exits

## Context

!agent: Scan the codebase and document relevant files, patterns, and constraints here.

## Implementation Checklist

!agent: Break down the implementation into steps and fill out this checklist
- [ ] Step 1: Description
- [ ] Step 2: Description
- [ ] Step 3: Description

## Questions

!agent: Identify any open questions or ambiguities that need clarification
- Question 1?
- Question 2?

## Log

!agent: Update this log as you work on this change, recording significant progress, decisions, and blockers
- YYYY-MM-DD: Change created

## Changelog Type

To be determined during implementation (feature/fix/change/deprecated/removed/security/internal)

qmdd.title: Add status command
qmdd.body:
## Goal
Add a `status` command to pebbles that shows the current workspace context including the active change ID and resolved VCS, similar to how `git status` shows repository state.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CLI Interface
  - [ ] Add `status` subcommand (no arguments)
  - [ ] Works only when in a pebbles workspace
  - [ ] Error if not in a workspace

- [ ] Display Information
  - [ ] Show current PEBBLES_CHANGE ID
  - [ ] Show change title and current status
  - [ ] Show resolved VCS (git/jujutsu)
  - [ ] Show workspace directory path

- [ ] Error Handling
  - [ ] Clear error when not in a workspace
  - [ ] Show helpful message about creating workspaces with `pebbles start --isolate`
  - [ ] Verify pebbles repository is initialized

## Context

### Similar Commands:
- `pebbles current` - Shows current workspace change ID only
- `pebbles show` - Shows full change details (requires ID)

### Key Implementation Patterns:
- VCS detection via `crate::vcs::detect_vcs()`
- Workspace ID via `vcs.current_workspace_id()`
- Repository access via `ChangeRepository::open()`
- ID resolution in commands/mod.rs `resolve_id()` function

### Related Files:
- src/cli.rs - Add Status variant to Commands enum
- src/main.rs - Route Status command
- src/commands/mod.rs - Export status module
- src/commands/current.rs - Reference for workspace detection
- src/vcs/mod.rs - VCS detection interface

## Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Step 1: Add CLI command structure
  - Add `Status` variant to `Commands` enum in src/cli.rs
  - No arguments needed (reads from environment/workspace)
  - Update src/main.rs to route the command

- [ ] Step 2: Create status command module
  - Create src/commands/status.rs
  - Detect VCS and get current workspace ID
  - Fetch change details from repository
  - Format and display the information

- [ ] Step 3: Implement workspace detection
  - Use VCS trait to detect current workspace
  - Extract change ID from workspace name
  - Handle case when not in a workspace

- [ ] Step 4: Format output
  - Show change ID with unique prefix highlighting
  - Show title, status (with color), and VCS
  - Show workspace directory path

- [ ] Step 5: Update module exports
  - Export status module in src/commands/mod.rs
  - Add `pub use status::status;`

- [ ] Step 6: Testing
  - Test in workspace with git
  - Test in workspace with jujutsu
  - Test outside workspace (should error)
  - Test in non-pebbles repo (should error)

## Example Output

When in workspace:
```
Workspace: ws-abc12/
Change: abc12 - Add status command
Status: in_progress
VCS: jujutsu
```

When not in workspace:
```
Error: Not in a pebbles workspace

Run 'pebbles start --isolate <id>' to create a workspace.
```

## Log
- 2026-02-19: Planning completed - acceptance criteria and implementation checklist defined

## Changelog Type
feature

zxun.title: Implement Releases feature for Pebbles
zxun.body:
## Implementation Plan

### Overview
Add Releases feature to Pebbles for organizing changes into versioned milestones (like Docket).

### Decisions Made
- Release IDs: Semver format (e.g., '1.0.0')
- One target_release per change
- Auto-create 'unscheduled' release on init
- Store release events in same events table
- Auto-generate changelog on ship (stdout or --file)
- Copy Docket's release show display exactly
- Support ISO 8601 + relative dates for target_date
- Delete: Error if has changes, --force (cascade), --reset (move to unscheduled)

### Implementation Phases
1. Core Data Models & Storage - Release struct, ReleaseStatus enum, extend Change and EventType
2. Validation & Utilities - semver validation, release sorting
3. CLI Commands - release new/list/show/edit/schedule/activate/freeze/ship/cancel/delete
4. UI Enhancements - show target_release in change details, --release filter
5. Changelog Generation - group by ChangelogType on ship
6. Migration & Edge Cases - auto-create unscheduled, handle existing data

### Files to Modify
- src/models.rs (Release, ReleaseStatus, Change.target_release, EventType variants)
- src/db.rs (releases HashMap, CRUD methods)
- src/repository.rs (ensure_unscheduled_release)
- src/cli.rs (Release command group)
- src/commands/release/*.rs (7 new files)
- src/commands/show.rs, list.rs (add release display)
- src/commands/init.rs (call ensure_unscheduled_release)
- src/release.rs (new file for validation)

### Dependencies
- semver crate
- chrono-english or similar for relative dates

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Can create releases with semver IDs
- [ ] Changes can be assigned to releases via schedule command
- [ ] Release list shows progress (completed/total)
- [ ] Release show displays changes grouped by status (copy Docket)
- [ ] Ship command generates changelog
- [ ] Delete release handles assigned changes properly
- [ ] Unscheduled release auto-created on init
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests for release functionality

2myi.title: Add intake command
2myi.body:
## Log
- 2026-02-19: Implemented intake command with file/STDIN reading, opencode integration, tests, and documentation

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **CLI Interface**
   - [x] Add `intake` command to CLI with optional file path argument
   - [x] Support reading from STDIN when no file is provided
   - [x] Support reading from file when path is provided

2. **Text Processing**
   - [x] Read content from file or STDIN
   - [x] Parse text to identify a single top-level issue
   - [x] Identify all child issues from the text
   - [x] Pass structured data to opencode with instructions to create issues

3. **Issue Creation**
   - [x] Launch opencode with `/intake` command
   - [x] Create a single parent/top-level change
   - [x] Create all other changes as children of the parent change
   - [x] Each change should have a title extracted from the text

4. **Error Handling**
   - [x] Handle file not found errors gracefully
   - [x] Handle empty input gracefully
   - [x] Validate pebbles repository is initialized

hoy9.title: Add support for issues with dependencies
hoy9.body: Docket would call these epics, not the world's biggest fan of epic, but we want to track the idea of dependencies between issues and also show that in the list view. This means the list view is going to need a tree mode. so the "list" view should probably not draw boxes around everything - the table code should not have borders but still help us control the output (i.e. the width.)

oija.title: Add output feedback to opencode run command
oija.body:
## Goal
Add output feedback to the 'pebbles start' command so users know when opencode is working.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Add --print-logs flag to pebbles start command
- [x] --print-logs flag passes through to opencode run command
- [x] On non-Unix systems, stdout/stderr is inherited when --print-logs is set

## Implementation Notes

Added a new --print-logs flag to the 'pebbles start' command:

### Changes Made:
1. **src/cli.rs:52-54** - Added --print-logs flag to Start command with help text
2. **src/main.rs:27,34** - Updated Start command handler to pass print_logs parameter
3. **src/commands/start.rs:13,78-80,84,89-101** - 
   - Updated function signature to accept print_logs parameter
   - Pass --print-logs to opencode when flag is set
   - On non-Unix systems, inherit stdout/stderr when flag is set

### Usage:
```
pebbles start <id> --print-logs
```

This will show opencode's output/logs to the console instead of running silently.

## Log
- 2026-02-19: Added --print-logs flag to pebbles start command

0zu0.title: add plan command
0zu0.body:
## Goal
Implement a `plan` command for pebbles that helps users break down and enrich existing issues by invoking opencode's /plan command.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Command opens and reads the issue
- [x] Displays change details before invoking opencode
- [x] Invokes `opencode run /plan` for the user
- [x] Works with optional ID or current workspace
- [x] Creates `.opencode/commands/plan.md` prompt

## Context

The plan command should:
1. Display the change details
2. Launch opencode with `opencode run /plan`
3. Set PEBBLES_CHANGE and PEBBLES_VCS environment variables
4. The plan.md prompt guides the LLM through:
   - Reviewing acceptance criteria
   - Scanning the codebase for context
   - Creating an implementation checklist
   - Identifying dependencies
   - Updating the change body

## Implementation Notes

Created:
- `src/commands/plan.rs` - Displays change and launches opencode
- `.opencode/commands/plan.md` - LLM prompt for planning workflow

Log:
- 2026-02-19: Implemented plan command that invokes opencode run /plan
- 2026-02-19: Created plan.md with structured planning workflow

fq24.title: abcd
fq24.body:
We need a CLI command that lets us edit the title of an issue.

## Implementation

The functionality already exists via the `pebbles update` command:

```
pebbles update <id> --title "new title"
```

## Log
- 2026-02-18: Verified `pebbles update --title` already provides this functionality
- 2026-02-18: Fixed pre-existing clippy warnings across the codebase
